key_insight: A hybrid SSNEB workflow that combines pretrained ML potentials with DFT can map minimum energy pathways much faster while preserving first-principles accuracy.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical route to accelerate transition-state and phase-transform calculations for solid-state materials design and reliability studies.
method: Use SSNEB to optimize reaction pathways between metastable states.
Integrate pretrained EquiformerV2 and eSEN models for energy, force, and stress evaluations.
Fall back on DFT within the hybrid framework to ensure pathway fidelity.
evidence_signal: Across three solid-state systems (CsPbI3, GaN, TiO2), the approach matched DFT-predicted pathways and achieved up to a 7-fold speedup.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and transferability of the pretrained ML models, so new materials may still require careful validation against DFT.
paper_title: Machine Learning Accelerated SSNEB for Efficient Minimum Energy Pathway Calculations
